topic: Resting heart rate over 70 — why that's a warning sign
caption:
Is your heart secretly working overtime? ❤️‍🩹 A resting heart rate consistently over 70 BPM isn't just a number; it's a silent signal your body might be under strain, potentially shortening your heart's lifespan. Your RHR reflects how hard your heart works at rest. For most healthy adults, it should ideally be between 60-100 BPM, but studies show consistently above 70 can indicate reduced cardiovascular fitness or underlying issues. 📉 A constantly elevated RHR forces your heart to beat more times over your lifetime, potentially accelerating wear and tear on cardiac tissue and increasing risk for conditions like heart disease and stroke. 💔 Factors like chronic stress, dehydration, lack of sleep, and even certain medications can temporarily raise your RHR. Persistent elevation warrants a closer look at your lifestyle and health markers. 🏃‍♀️💧 But don't panic! Simple lifestyle changes like regular exercise, stress management, and adequate hydration can significantly lower your RHR, improving heart health and longevity. Consult your doctor if it persists. 🩺

💾 Save this post to keep your heart health in check. Share it with a friend who's curious about their heart rate. Follow @healthrecode for daily science-backed insights into your body's signals. Don't forget, understanding your body is the first step to a healthier life!
